varieties.name: Prunus subhirtella 'Pendula Rubra'
varieties.traits: Weeping form, lanceolate leaves, reddish-brown bark
varieties.availability: Fleming's Nursery Queensland, Westlake Nursery, Hello Hello Plants Melbourne (ships to QLD)

varieties.name: Prunus subhirtella 'Pendula Rosea'
varieties.origin: Japan, introduced UK ~1870, also called 'Pendula' or flesh-pink weeping cherry
varieties.traits: Graceful umbrella weeping form, reaches 4-6m height and width
varieties.availability: Widely available in Australian nurseries including Queensland outlets

varieties.name: Prunus subhirtella 'Pendula Plena Rosea'
varieties.origin: Japan, introduced by Collingwood Ingram 1928 from Heian-Jingu temple, Kyoto
varieties.traits: Double-flowered weeping form, delicately twisted petals
varieties.yield_notes: Abundant mid-spring flowering, endures longer than other cherries
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries Melbourne and Sydney (limited QLD availability)

varieties.name: Prunus subhirtella 'Falling Snow'
varieties.traits: Compact symmetrical weeping form, cascades almost straight down, 3-4m height

varieties.name: Prunus subhirtella 'Subhirtella Alba' (Sub Alba)
varieties.traits: Strong vigorous growth, can reach 3-3.5m width, hardy to wind and heat
varieties.yield_notes: Overwhelming flower production when mature, takes 2-3 years to establish strong head
varieties.availability: Hello Hello Plants, specialty nurseries Melbourne, Victoria (limited humid subtropical climates stock)

varieties.name: Prunus subhirtella 'Subhirtella Rosea' (Sub Rosea)
varieties.traits: Large spreading tree, can reach 3-3.5m wide head creating canopy effect
varieties.yield_notes: Spectacular full head of flowers, very impressive mature specimens
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries Victoria, NSW (occasional humid subtropical climates availability)

varieties.name: Prunus × subhirtella 'Autumnalis Rosea'
varieties.traits: Unique characteristic - flowers sporadically in autumn AND spring, dense rounded habit, 4-6m height
varieties.yield_notes: Extended flowering season (autumn + spring), pea-sized black fruits attract birds
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries Victoria, NSW, some Queensland outlets

varieties.name: Prunus serrulata 'Cheals Weeping' (Kiku-Shidare-Zakura)
varieties.origin: Japan, cultivated variety also called 'Cheals Double Pink'
varieties.traits: Grafted weeping standard (grafted at ~2m), thin delicate weeping branches, 3-4m tall, compact form
varieties.availability: Specialty Trees Melbourne (ships to QLD), humid subtropical climates Plant Nursery, Nursery Near Me

varieties.name: Prunus serrulata 'Kanzan'
varieties.origin: Japan, one of the showiest flowering cherries
varieties.traits: Upright vase-shaped tree, vigorous growth, narrow at first then spreading with maturity, 6-8m height
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy flowering, one of most spectacular displays, yellow-orange autumn color

varieties.name: Prunus serrulata 'Shimidsu Sakura' (Shogetsu)
varieties.traits: Spreading growth habit, wide horizontal branches, pink-bronze young foliage
varieties.availability: Garden Express, specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Prunus serrulata 'Tai Haku' (Great White Cherry)
varieties.origin: Japan, largest blooms of all cherries
varieties.traits: Spreading small tree, sometimes grafted as standard at ~2m, ascending branches, copper-bronze young leaves

varieties.name: Prunus serrulata 'Mt. Fuji' (Shirotae)
varieties.origin: Japan, also known as Shirotae Cherry or Mount Fuji Cherry
varieties.traits: Grafted to 2m, reaches 3m tall and wide at maturity, fresh green foliage turns orange-red in autumn
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific flowering, multi-season interest with autumn color
varieties.availability: Online Plants Australia (Melbourne Metro delivery), specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Prunus serrulata 'Amanogawa'
varieties.traits: Distinctive narrow columnar form, ideal for tight spaces, 6-8m tall but only 1-2m wide
varieties.availability: Plantmark wholesale nurseries Melbourne/Sydney/humid subtropical climates

varieties.name: Prunus glandulosa (Dwarf Flowering Almond/Chinese Bush Cherry)
varieties.origin: China and Japan, also called Chinese Plum or Dwarf Flowering Almond
varieties.traits: Dwarf flowering shrub 1-1.5m tall, suitable for pots and small spaces
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, bonsai suppliers

varieties.name: Prunus cerasoides (Wild Himalayan Cherry, Padam) 🌏
varieties.origin: Himalayas (Himachal Pradesh to Sikkim, Nepal, Bhutan), Myanmar, Thailand - INDIGENOUS TO INDIA
varieties.traits: Deciduous tree 3-10m (can reach 30m), glossy ringed bark, elliptic-lanceolate leaves with dentate margins
varieties.availability: Paudhshala India (Himalayan region), specialty nurseries in temperate hill stations, NOT available in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Prunus campanulata (Taiwan Cherry, Formosan Cherry)
varieties.origin: Taiwan, southern China (Guangdong, Fujian), Vietnam - subtropical native
varieties.traits: Small deciduous tree 3-8m (up to 10m), bell-shaped deep red-pink flowers, serrated bright green leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Tolerates heat and humidity better than P. subhirtella. Fastest growing cherry for warm climates. Considered invasive in New Zealand.
varieties.availability: Top Tropicals (subtropical regions), Green Garden Philippines, NOT commonly available in India

varieties.name: Malpighia emarginata (Barbados Cherry, Acerola, West Indian Cherry)
varieties.origin: Caribbean, South America (Lesser Antilles to Brazil), naturalized throughout tropics
varieties.traits: Evergreen shrub/small tree 2-3m (up to 6m), spreading branches on short trunk, glossy leaves
varieties.yield_notes: 62-82 pounds per season, fruit matures 22-25 days after flowering, multiple flushes with rain
varieties.growing_notes: Also called Malpighia glabra (wild ornamental species with less fruit). Grown commercially in Brazil (largest producer), India, Australia.
varieties.availability: KISSAN tropical monsoon climates (recommended for homesteads), Plants Corner India, Mahindra Nursery, tropical monsoon climates agricultural nurseries

varieties.name: Muntingia calabura (Manila Cherry, Philippine Cherry, Santol)
varieties.origin: Philippines native, cultivated throughout tropical Asia
varieties.traits: Fast-growing tropical tree, relatively easy to grow, evergreen
varieties.availability: Mahindra Nursery India, tropical fruit nurseries tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Eugenia aggregata (Cherry of the Rio Grande)
varieties.traits: Tropical evergreen shrub/small tree, Myrtaceae family (myrtle family - most tropical cherries)
varieties.availability: California Tropical Fruit Tree Nursery (ships to tropical Asia), specialty tropical fruit nurseries

varieties.name: Eugenia uniflora (Surinam Cherry, Pitanga)
varieties.traits: Tropical evergreen shrub, ornamental and productive
varieties.availability: Tropical fruit nurseries, some availability in South India

varieties.name: Eugenia reinwardtiana (Cedar Bay Cherry)
varieties.traits: Rainforest shrub/small tree, very slow-growing, prefers part shade
varieties.growing_notes: Highly rated for flavor similarity to temperate cherries. Productivity uncertain when fully mature. Needs patience.
varieties.availability: Australian tropical fruit nurseries, limited availability

varieties.name: Myrciaria cauliflora (Jaboticaba, Brazilian Grape)
varieties.traits: Unique tropical tree, fruits grow directly on trunk and branches (cauliflorous)
varieties.availability: Tropical fruit nurseries India, limited tropical monsoon climates availability

varieties.name: Grumichama (Eugenia brasiliensis)
varieties.traits: Tropical evergreen tree, considered 'tropical cherry'
varieties.availability: Tropical fruit nurseries
